cli.manual_review_warning: Warning: Higher severity rules (Critical/Medium) often require manual engineering review.
html.title: DockCheck — Analysis Report
html.no_issues_detected: No issues detected — all checks passed.
html.footer_desc: DockCheck — Static analysis tool for Docker environments
rule_desc_DF-001: Base image uses ':latest' tag or has no tag
rule_rec_DF-001: Pin your base image to a specific version (e.g. python:3.11-slim) to guarantee reproducibility.
rule_desc_DF-002: Container runs as root user (no USER instruction or USER root)
rule_rec_DF-002: Add a non-root USER instruction before CMD/ENTRYPOINT (e.g. RUN useradd -m appuser && USER appuser).
rule_desc_DF-003: Multiple consecutive RUN instructions create unnecessary layers
rule_rec_DF-003: Chain RUN commands with '&&' and '\' to reduce the number of image layers.
rule_desc_DF-004: ADD instruction used instead of COPY
rule_rec_DF-004: Replace ADD with COPY unless you specifically need ADD's tar-extraction or URL-fetching behaviour.
rule_rec_DF-005: Define a WORKDIR to set a clear working directory and avoid ambiguity in file paths.
rule_desc_DF-006: apt-get update and apt-get install in separate RUN instructions
rule_rec_DF-006: Combine 'apt-get update' and 'apt-get install' in a single RUN instruction to avoid stale package lists.
rule_desc_DF-007: No HEALTHCHECK instruction defined, preventing Docker from monitoring container health
rule_rec_DF-007: Add a HEALTHCHECK instruction to allow Docker to monitor the container's runtime state and restart it if unhealthy. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.
rule_desc_DF-008: apt-get install used without --no-install-recommends flag, pulling unnecessary packages
rule_rec_DF-008: Add '--no-install-recommends' to 'apt-get install' to minimise image size by only installing explicitly required packages. Source: 10 Docker Best Practices (0xfujin).
rule_desc_DF-009: pip install used without --no-cache-dir flag, leaving pip cache in the image layer
rule_rec_DF-009: Add '--no-cache-dir' to 'pip install' to avoid storing pip's download cache in the image, reducing final image size. Source: 10 Docker Best Practices (0xfujin).
rule_desc_IMG-001: Image has no labels (maintainer, version, etc.)
rule_rec_IMG-001: Add LABEL instructions for at least 'maintainer' and 'version' to improve traceability.
rule_desc_IMG-002: Image contains sensitive data in environment variables (password, secret, key)
rule_rec_IMG-002: Never store secrets in ENV instructions. Use Docker secrets, a .env file, or a secrets manager instead.
rule_desc_IMG-003: Image has an excessive number of layers (> 20)
rule_rec_IMG-003: Reduce the number of layers by combining RUN instructions and using multi-stage builds.
rule_rec_IMG-004: Ensure the image's USER is set to a non-root account in the Dockerfile.
rule_desc_IMG-005: Base image uses ':latest' tag or has no tag
rule_rec_IMG-005: Pin the base image to a specific digest or version tag.
rule_desc_DC-001: Service uses an image with ':latest' tag or no tag
rule_rec_DC-001: Pin service images to a specific version tag to ensure reproducible deployments.
rule_desc_DC-002: Service runs as root or has no 'user' defined
rule_rec_DC-002: Define a non-root 'user' for each service (e.g. user: '1000').
rule_desc_DC-003: Service exposes a plaintext password or secret in environment variables
rule_rec_DC-003: Use Docker secrets or a .env file instead of hardcoding credentials in docker-compose.yml.
rule_desc_DC-004: Service exposes a port on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) or a sensitive port
rule_rec_DC-004: Bind ports to a specific interface (e.g. 127.0.0.1:8080:80) and avoid exposing privileged ports externally.
rule_rec_DC-005: Review whether duplicate service images are intentional; consider merging redundant services.
rule_desc_DC-006: Service runs in privileged mode, granting full root capabilities on the host
rule_rec_DC-006: Remove 'privileged: true' and use fine-grained 'cap_add' for only the capabilities the container requires. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.
rule_desc_DC-007: Service mounts a volume without read-only flag for non-data directories
rule_rec_DC-007: Mount volumes as read-only (:ro) wherever write access is not needed to reduce the container's attack surface. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.
rule_desc_SW-001: Service has no explicit replica count defined in deploy configuration
rule_rec_SW-001: Define an explicit 'replicas' value under 'deploy' to control service scaling and avoid ambiguity.
rule_desc_SW-002: Service has no CPU or memory limits defined (resources.limits)
rule_rec_SW-002: Define 'resources.limits' with CPU and memory caps to prevent a single service from monopolising cluster resources.
rule_desc_SW-003: Service has no CPU or memory reservations defined (resources.reservations)
rule_rec_SW-003: Define 'resources.reservations' to guarantee a minimum allocation and improve scheduling predictability.
rule_desc_SW-004: Service has no restart policy defined or no bounded max_attempts
rule_rec_SW-004: Define a 'restart_policy' with 'condition', 'delay', and bounded 'max_attempts' to avoid infinite restart loops destabilising nodes.
rule_desc_SW-005: Service has no placement constraints defined
rule_rec_SW-005: Use 'placement.constraints' to control service scheduling across nodes (e.g., pin database services to specific nodes).
rule_desc_SW-006: Service has no rolling update configuration (update_config)
rule_rec_SW-006: Define 'update_config' with 'parallelism' and 'delay' to ensure zero-downtime deployments during rolling updates.
rule_desc_SW-007: Service passes sensitive data (passwords, secrets, API keys) via environment variables instead of Docker Swarm secrets
rule_rec_SW-007: Use the Docker Swarm secrets mechanism instead of environment variables for sensitive data. Env vars may be visible in logs or 'docker inspect' output.
rule_desc_SW-008: Service uses ':latest' tag or unversioned image, undermining deployment reproducibility
rule_rec_SW-008: Pin service images to a specific version tag or digest to guarantee reproducible deployments across all Swarm nodes.
rule_desc_SW-009: Service relies on the default network instead of an explicitly defined overlay network
rule_rec_SW-009: Attach services to explicitly defined overlay networks to enforce proper service isolation and avoid unintended cross-service communication.
rule_desc_SW-010: Service has no health check defined, preventing Swarm from detecting and replacing unhealthy tasks
rule_rec_SW-010: Define a 'healthcheck' with 'test', 'interval', 'timeout', and 'retries' to allow Swarm's scheduler to detect and replace unhealthy tasks.
rule_desc_SW-011: Service has no logging driver configuration defined, making log aggregation and monitoring difficult
rule_rec_SW-011: Define a 'logging' section with an appropriate driver (json-file, syslog, fluentd) and 'max-size'/'max-file' options to enable structured log collection and prevent runaway disk usage. Source: AccuWeb Swarm Best Practices, IBM Docker Security.
rule_desc_SW-012: Service runs in privileged mode, granting unrestricted access to the host system
rule_rec_SW-012: Remove 'privileged: true' and use the minimum required capabilities via 'cap_add'. Privileged containers bypass all security isolation. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.
rule_desc_DF-010: apt-get install used without footprint cleanup
rule_rec_DF-010: Add 'rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' after 'apt-get install' to reduce image tracking bloat.
rule_rec_DF-011: Use 'apk add --no-cache' instead of maintaining apk caches.
rule_desc_DF-012: yum install used without repository cleanup
rule_rec_DF-012: Add 'yum clean all' after 'yum install' to purge fetched package caches.
rule_rec_DF-013: Use 'curl -fSL' to ensure Docker builds fail fast if the remote asset returns HTTP errors.
rule_desc_DF-014: wget used without quiet-output stream bindings
rule_rec_DF-014: Use 'wget -qO-' to streamline binary streaming securely in build contexts.
rule_rec_DF-015: Do not use 'sudo' in Dockerfiles. Explicitly switch users via the 'USER' command.
rule_desc_DF-016: No EXPOSE network port mappings declared
rule_rec_DF-016: Explicitly define 'EXPOSE <port>' to improve port discovery and document container intent.
rule_desc_DF-018: Inline path traversals (cd) rather than deterministic WORKDIR
rule_rec_DF-018: Use explicit 'WORKDIR /path' statements instead of calling 'cd' to maintain execution determinism.
rule_desc_DF-019: npm install used without clearing node caching
rule_rec_DF-019: Execute 'npm cache clean --force' after 'npm install' to destroy temporary JavaScript dependency layers.
rule_desc_DC-009: Service missing a localized healthcheck configuration
rule_rec_DC-009: Define healthchecks directly in Compose or enforce them at the Image level.
rule_desc_DC-010: Service assigns container to host network mode
rule_rec_DC-010: Avoid 'network_mode: host'. Use mapped ports to preserve software-defined-network isolation.
rule_desc_DC-011: Service breaks PID namespace via host mapping
rule_rec_DC-011: Remove 'pid: host'. Sharing the host process ID space allows container root escalation easily.
rule_desc_DC-012: Service ignores structured resource memory/CPU limitations
rule_rec_DC-012: Dictate execution footprints through 'deploy.resources.limits' to avoid node locking failures.
rule_desc_DC-014: SYS_ADMIN or ALL capability escalation granted
rule_rec_DC-014: Do not grant root-equivalent system capabilities such as 'SYS_ADMIN' globally via 'cap_add'.
rule_desc_DC-015: Inter-Process Communication attached directly to Host
rule_rec_DC-015: Avoid 'ipc: host' to ensure memory segment namespaces remain sealed against the hosting system.
rule_desc_DC-016: Fixed deployment bounded by hardcoded MAC identifiers
rule_rec_DC-016: Avoid pinning strict 'mac_address' configurations as it impedes scheduling fluidity globally.
rule_desc_DC-017: DNS queries strictly overridden skipping internal resolvers
rule_rec_DC-017: Refrain from hardcoding public DNS references (like 8.8.8.8) to maintain resolution independence.
rule_rec_SW-013: Implement 'stop_grace_period' dictations to perform safe termination signaling in clustered tears.
rule_desc_SW-014: Swarm routing mesh bypassed using internal dnsrr mode
rule_rec_SW-014: Use 'vip' (Virtual IP) mode instead of 'dnsrr' to retain layer-4 load-balancing topologies.
rule_desc_SW-015: Update ordering follows default stop-first pipeline causing down-time
rule_rec_SW-015: Configure 'update_config.order' as 'start-first' for automated Zero-Downtime rollouts.
rule_desc_SW-016: Restart temporal delays unbounded or suspiciously brief
rule_rec_SW-016: Include a minimum temporal 'delay' variable representing execution recovery to avoid failure loop spinbacks.
rule_desc_SW-017: Swarm deployment volume omits constraint isolation typing
rule_rec_SW-017: Use explicit volume structures ('type: volume|bind') when deploying storage to clusters.
rule_desc_IMG-006: Total image payload significantly exceeds 1GB limit
rule_rec_IMG-006: Use multi-stage builds alongside minimal architectural footprints targeting sub-500MB sizing constructs.
rule_desc_IMG-008: Scratch infrastructure defaults silently back to unregulated ROOT mappings
rule_rec_IMG-008: Even scratch deployments executing native go-binaries require constrained isolated UID mapping identities injected post-build.
rule_desc_IMG-009: Production configurations rely implicitly upon 'latest' tag allocations
rule_rec_IMG-009: Immutable images must omit the 'latest' string mapping internally to preserve determinism validation logic.
rule_desc_IMG-010: Constructed object artifacts miss active daemon process triggers (CMD/ENTRYPOINT)
rule_rec_IMG-010: Ensure an explicit execution directive dictates parameter parsing behaviors using static ENTRYPOINT formats.
rule_desc_DF-020: System-wide package updates (e.g., apt-get upgrade, apk upgrade) are used
rule_rec_DF-020: Do not upgrade system packages in Dockerfiles; rely on updated base images instead. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.7
rule_rec_DF-021: Explicitly remove setuid/setgid binaries during build (e.g., RUN find / -xdev -perm /6000 -type f -exec chmod a-s {} \;). Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.8
rule_desc_DF-022: Dockerfile uses ARG for sensitive data
rule_rec_DF-022: Do not use ARG for secrets. Use Docker BuildKit --secret instead. Source: Docker Official Security Best Practices
rule_desc_DF-023: ENTRYPOINT is in shell form rather than exec form
rule_rec_DF-023: Use the exec form of ENTRYPOINT (e.g., ENTRYPOINT ["executable", "param1"]) to ensure signals are properly passed. Source: Docker Official Best Practices
rule_desc_DF-024: CMD is in shell form rather than exec form
rule_rec_DF-024: Use the exec form of CMD to avoid wrapping the process in a shell. Source: Docker Official Best Practices
rule_rec_DF-025: Containers should not run SSH daemons. Use 'docker exec' for container access. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_DF-026: wget or curl is used without a checksum validation pipe (e.g., sha256sum)
rule_rec_DF-026: Always validate the checksum of downloaded binaries to prevent supply chain attacks. Source: SLSA Supply Chain Security Guidelines
rule_desc_DF-027: apk add is used without pinning package versions
rule_rec_DF-027: Pin package versions in 'apk add' (e.g., apk add package=1.2.3) to ensure deterministic builds. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_DF-028: apt-get install is used without pinning package versions
rule_rec_DF-028: Pin package versions in 'apt-get install' (e.g., apt-get install package=1.2.3) to ensure deterministic builds. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_DF-029: HEALTHCHECK interval is missing or set to zero
rule_rec_DF-029: HEALTHCHECK instructions must specify an interval greater than 0 to be effective. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.6
rule_desc_DF-030: COPY is followed by a RUN chown command
rule_rec_DF-030: Use the '--chown' flag in the COPY instruction (e.g., COPY --chown=user:group src dest) to reduce image layer size. Source: Docker Official Best Practices
rule_desc_DF-031: Base image is pulled from an untrusted generic public registry path
rule_rec_DF-031: Avoid using generic untrusted images. Prefer Official Images, Verified Publishers, or your own private registry. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.2
rule_desc_DF-032: Dockerfile lacks multi-stage build structure for compiled languages
rule_rec_DF-032: Use multi-stage builds to compile code in one stage and copy only the final binary to the production stage. Source: Docker Official Best Practices
rule_desc_DF-033: User is switched to root later in the Dockerfile
rule_rec_DF-033: Do not switch back to the root user after setting a non-root USER. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_DF-034: Use of unencrypted HTTP protocols in package managers
rule_rec_DF-034: Ensure package manager repositories use HTTPS to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks during build. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_rec_IMG-011: Add a HEALTHCHECK instruction to the Dockerfile. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.6
rule_desc_IMG-012: Image signed by Docker Content Trust / Cosign is missing
rule_rec_IMG-012: Enable Docker Content Trust or use Cosign to sign images. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.5
rule_rec_IMG-013: Use COPY instead of ADD to prevent automatic extraction or remote URL downloads. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.3
rule_desc_IMG-014: Image history contains system upgrade commands
rule_rec_IMG-014: Do not upgrade system packages during build. Rebuild from a patched base image instead. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.7
rule_desc_IMG-016: Image history contains sensitive keywords (password, secret)
rule_rec_IMG-016: Do not store secrets in image history or layers. Use secret management tools. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.10
rule_desc_IMG-017: USER is explicitly set to root or 0
rule_rec_IMG-017: Set the USER to a non-privileged account. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark 4.1
rule_desc_IMG-018: Image has suspiciously large layers (> 500MB per layer)
rule_rec_IMG-018: Break down large layers or use multi-stage builds to optimize image size. Source: Docker Official Best Practices
rule_desc_IMG-020: Image exposes ports on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) in Expose configurations
rule_rec_IMG-020: Do not bind exposed ports to 0.0.0.0 directly in the image metadata. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_IMG-022: Image configuration contains debugging environment variables
rule_rec_IMG-022: Remove debugging environment variables (like DEBUG=1) from production images. Source: OWASP Docker Security
rule_desc_IMG-023: Image lacks ENTRYPOINT but has CMD, or vice-versa
rule_rec_IMG-023: Define an ENTRYPOINT to make the container executable, and use CMD for default arguments. Source: Docker Official Best Practices
rule_desc_IMG-025: Image metadata contains overly permissive Volumes without security profiles
rule_rec_IMG-025: Limit declared volumes and enforce read-only where possible. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_IMG-026: Image layers contain wget/curl without checksum validation
rule_rec_IMG-026: Validate fetched binaries via checksum to protect against supply chain tampering. Source: SLSA
rule_rec_DC-018: Drop all Linux capabilities by default and only add back what is strictly necessary. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_rec_DC-019: Prevent privilege escalation by explicitly setting no-new-privileges:true. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_DC-020: Service mounts .env or sensitive files as volumes
rule_rec_DC-020: Do not mount sensitive files into the container. Use Docker Secrets instead. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_DC-021: Service does not configure a custom network
rule_rec_DC-021: Use custom user-defined networks for strict service isolation instead of the default bridge. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_rec_DC-022: Never mount the Docker socket inside a container unless absolutely necessary, as it grants full root access to the host. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_rec_DC-023: Set the container's root filesystem to read-only to prevent tampering. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_rec_DC-024: Avoid using explicit bridge mode; rely on named overlay or bridge networks. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_DC-025: Service mounts the host root / directory
rule_rec_DC-025: Do not mount the host root filesystem into a container. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_rec_DC-026: Do not mount the host /etc directory into a container. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_DC-027: Service uses env_file instead of Docker secrets
rule_rec_DC-027: Use Docker Secrets for sensitive configuration rather than env_files. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_DC-028: Service lacks a logging driver configuration with max-size
rule_rec_DC-028: Configure log rotation with max-size to prevent unbounded log growth. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_DC-029: Service tmpfs mounts lack explicit size limits
rule_rec_DC-029: Always specify a size limit for tmpfs mounts to prevent memory exhaustion. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_rec_DC-030: Avoid binding container services to privileged host ports below 1024. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_DC-031: Service lacks explicit depends_on for startup ordering
rule_rec_DC-031: Use depends_on to strictly define startup ordering for interrelated services. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_DC-032: Service uses cgroup_parent which can bypass isolation
rule_rec_DC-032: Do not override cgroup_parent unless specifically required for custom orchestration. Source: Docker Security
rule_desc_DC-033: Service defines userns_mode: host breaking user namespace isolation
rule_rec_DC-033: Avoid disabling user namespace isolation via userns_mode: host. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_SW-018: Swarm manager auto-lock mode not explicitly handled
rule_rec_SW-018: Enable autolock on swarm managers to encrypt the Raft log. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_rec_SW-019: Set driver_opts encrypted: true on overlay networks to secure data-plane traffic. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_SW-020: Swarm service uses host-bound volume mounts
rule_rec_SW-020: Avoid binding host directories directly; use named volumes for swarm mobility. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_SW-021: Swarm nodes missing placement constraints / role separation
rule_rec_SW-021: Use placement constraints to ensure services land on appropriate worker nodes, isolating managers. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_SW-022: Swarm manager binding to untrusted / wildcard interfaces (0.0.0.0)
rule_rec_SW-022: Bind swarm endpoints to specific trusted management interfaces. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_SW-023: Swarm service does not declare rolling update parallelism limits
rule_rec_SW-023: Define update_config with parallelism limits to prevent cluster-wide outages during updates. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_SW-024: Swarm secret mapped to container filesystem improperly
rule_rec_SW-024: Ensure swarm secrets are managed by the orchestrator and mounted in tmpfs. Source: NIST SP 800-190
rule_desc_SW-025: Swarm service exposes management API ports
rule_rec_SW-025: Do not expose ports 2377, 7946, or 4789 on service containers. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_SW-026: Swarm service lacks declarative CPU limits
rule_rec_SW-026: Set CPU resource limits on swarm services to prevent noisy neighbor issues. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_SW-027: Swarm service lacks declarative memory limits
rule_rec_SW-027: Set memory resource limits on swarm services to prevent Out-Of-Memory host crashes. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_SW-028: Swarm service deployment uses global mode
rule_rec_SW-028: Use replicated mode instead of global mode unless strictly required for DaemonSets. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_SW-029: Swarm config mapped via env variables instead of Docker Configs
rule_rec_SW-029: Use Docker Configs for application configuration instead of injecting large environment payloads. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_desc_SW-030: Swarm service ignores endpoint_mode dnsrr for internal discovery
rule_rec_SW-030: Consider dnsrr endpoint_mode for internal headless services instead of VIP. Source: Docker Best Practices
rule_rec_SW-031: Do not grant all capabilities to swarm services. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_desc_SW-032: Swarm services bypassing routing mesh using explicit host ports
rule_rec_SW-032: Avoid using 'mode: host' for port publishing; rely on the ingress routing mesh. Source: Docker Security
rule_desc_SW-033: Swarm service deployed without healthcheck propagation
rule_rec_SW-033: Ensure healthchecks are defined so the orchestrator can reschedule unhealthy containers. Source: CIS Docker Benchmark
rule_rec_DF-036: Always use absolute paths for your WORKDIR to prevent path resolution ambiguities.
rule_rec_DF-039: Pin versions when using gem install to ensure deterministic builds (e.g., gem install mygem:1.0.0).
rule_rec_DF-040: Pin versions when using npm install to ensure deterministic builds (e.g., npm install mypackage@1.0.0).
rule_rec_DF-042: Add the -y flag to yum install to ensure the build doesn't hang waiting for user input.
rule_rec_DF-044: Pin versions when using yum install to ensure deterministic builds.
rule_rec_DF-045: Add the -y flag to zypper install to ensure the build doesn't hang.
rule_desc_DF-047: zypper clean missing after zypper commands
rule_rec_DF-047: Clean the zypper cache after installing packages to reduce image size.
rule_rec_DF-048: Pin versions when using zypper install to ensure deterministic builds.
rule_rec_DF-049: Add the -y flag to dnf install to ensure the build doesn't hang.
rule_desc_DF-050: dnf clean all missing after dnf commands
rule_rec_DF-050: Clean the dnf cache after installing packages to reduce image size.
rule_rec_DF-051: Pin versions when using dnf install to ensure deterministic builds.
rule_rec_DF-052: Only the last HEALTHCHECK instruction takes effect. Remove duplicates to avoid confusion.
rule_rec_DF-053: Ensure EXPOSE ports are valid integers between 1 and 65535.
rule_desc_DF-054: COPY with more than 2 arguments doesn't end with /
rule_rec_DF-054: When copying multiple files, the destination must be a directory and end with a trailing slash.
rule_desc_DF-055: COPY --from references a non-existent stage alias
rule_rec_DF-055: Ensure that the stage referenced in COPY --from matches a previous FROM ... AS <stage>.
rule_desc_DF-056: FROM stage aliases are not unique
rule_rec_DF-056: Use unique names for each FROM ... AS <stage> to avoid overriding stages.
rule_desc_DF-058: Use of apt instead of apt-get or apt-cache
rule_rec_DF-058: The 'apt' command is meant for interactive use. Use 'apt-get' or 'apt-cache' in Dockerfiles.
rule_desc_IMG-028: Image does not have an SBOM attached
rule_rec_IMG-028: Generate and attach a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) using tools like Syft or Docker Scout.
rule_desc_IMG-029: Image does not have a signed SLSA provenance attestation
rule_rec_IMG-029: Implement build provenance using Docker Buildx or external SLSA tools to guarantee build origin.
rule_desc_IMG-030: Image uses a full OS base when a distroless or slim variant would suffice
rule_rec_IMG-030: Use slim, alpine, or distroless base images to minimize the attack surface.
rule_desc_IMG-031: Image layers contain debugging tools (strace, gdb, curl, wget)
rule_rec_IMG-031: Remove debugging tools from production images or use multi-stage builds to exclude them.
rule_desc_IMG-032: Image layers contain a shell (bash/sh) in a production runtime image
rule_rec_IMG-032: Consider using distroless images that lack a shell to prevent remote code execution post-exploitation.
rule_desc_IMG-033: Image layers contain package managers (apt, apk, yum, dnf)
rule_rec_IMG-033: Remove package managers from production images to prevent attackers from downloading tools.
